measured_entities.interpretation: Gold-standard layer for heat strain, fever/contextual illness, and circadian thermal rhythm when measured with validated core methods.
guardrails: Do not infer fever from exercise-induced hyperthermia.
Compare body sites only with calibration.

measured_entities.interpretation: Useful for sleep transition/circadian proxy and thermal comfort, not clinical fever diagnosis.
guardrails: Wrist/skin temperature alone is not validated for fever diagnosis.

measured_entities.interpretation: Strong cross-modal heat strain branch when CBT, HR, environment and symptoms/performance are measured together.
guardrails: Thermal discomfort is not identical to heat strain.

measured_entities.interpretation: Wearable/distal temperature can support menstrual-cycle context and should not be misread as infection without inflammatory/symptom context.
Medication effects can confound heat strain and thermal response.
guardrails: Do not generalize all medications as raising CBT.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Fever can be reliably detected with wrist temperature alone.
Low temperature trend can diagnose thyroid dysfunction.

summary.overall_assessment: Temperature tree is usable. Strong for heat strain, menstrual-cycle temperature, and sleep transition; weaker for astronaut-specific thermoregulation and fever detection via wearables.
